---
title: 'Class Assignment: What is the gospel, according to Matthew?'
date: '2024-06-09T12:46:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: false
categories:
  - 'Gospel'
---

<p>&nbsp;Assignment: Without referencing any other New Testament writer, but you can reference any Hebrew Bible writer that the gospel author himself references, what is the gospel according to...&nbsp;</p><div><br /></div><br /><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span class="MsoBookTitle"><span style="font-size: 16pt; line-height: 42.6667px;">Gospel Reading: Matthew<o:p></o:p></span></span></p><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12pt;">Darrell Wolfe</span></p><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12pt; line-height: 32px; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Literature, The King’s University<o:p></o:p></span></p><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12pt; line-height: 32px; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Life of Jesus (BIBL-2302)<o:p></o:p></span></p><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12pt; line-height: 32px; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Professor Jason Moraff<o:p></o:p></span></p><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12pt; line-height: 32px; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">October 25, 2020</span></p><p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12pt; line-height: 32px; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><br /></span></p><h1>Questions<o:p></o:p></h1><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->1.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Lilies of the Field (Matthew 6:28-30). Even Solomon was not dressed like lilies? I get that the point is not to worry. But I feel like there is something I’m missing. How are lilies and clothes comparable? How are Lilies adorned? There is something deeper here. Maybe if I know something about lilies, I could learn something about God’s provision?<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->2.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 8 - I still do not understand why the demons wanted to go into the pigs or why Jesus let them. I have theories, but nothing fully satisfying.<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->3.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 9 – How could we retool our churches to be hospitals for the sick rather than just schools for the healed?<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->4.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 17 – How often do I look down on “little ones”? Either actual children, finding even my own children annoying or distracting from my “real work”. Or spiritual children, those who “know less” than I do? If the least are greatest, am I seeing them through God’s eyes?<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->5.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 25 – I can’t reconcile the 10 virgins. Are they believers? But then they’d all be allowed in? Are they Christians but five of them are in name only? Not really saved? Is this parallel to the one taken the other left Jesus referred to at his coming?<o:p></o:p></p><h1>Observations<o:p></o:p></h1><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Matthew 1: Rahab and Ruth were both foreigners brought into the Kingdom of Heaven by forsaking all they had. Tamar and Bathsheba were women who had been wronged sexually. Mary was an Israelite who forsook her future to say yes to God and was perceived by others to be wronged sexually. These five women made the list of Jesus’ family<a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftn1" name="_ftnref1" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11pt; line-height: 15.6933px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[1]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>.<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 4 - Matthew summarizes Jesus’ entire life message as: “Repent for the kingdom of heaven is at hand” but not “Repent, receive forgiveness, go to heaven when you die”. Matthew 4:17<a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftn2" name="_ftnref2" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11pt; line-height: 15.6933px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[2]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 7 - Gentiles come in and sit with Abraham in the Kingdom. Not a new kingdom. Not a different kingdom. We come in; they do not come out. We are grafted into Israel; we are not a separate (<i>implied better</i>) nation. We are part of the same continuous story.<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 8 - Peter’s Mom gets sick even though he is serving Jesus. I guess life still happens when we choose to serve. We are not exempt.<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 8:20 - Jesus said he had nowhere to lay his head. Spangler and Tverberg pointed out that Jesus would have been an itinerate rabbi<a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftn3" name="_ftnref3" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11pt; line-height: 15.6933px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[3]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>. Therefore, Jesus is not saying he was “poor” he is saying that he was rejected by some of the places he traveled and wasn’t accepted into anyone’s home (as was the custom).<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 9 – Gentile churches today are often no more friendly to the spiritually sick than the pharisees that Jesus was confronting.<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: normal; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Ch. 10 – I often hesitate to accept hospitality from those I have served or helped. I think this a challenge for me to allow community into my life. Breathing in and out, I must serve but allow myself to be served (10:10 NLT). On that note, am I more afraid of what people think of me than what God thinks of me? Am I afraid of people and not afraid of God (10:28)?<o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="line-height: normal; margin-left: 0.25in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -0.25in;"><span style="font-family: Symbol;">&nbsp;</span>Ch. 26 – Let this cup pass… Even Jesus didn’t always “want” to do God’s will, but he willed to do God’s will. The emotions need not be present for obedience.<o:p></o:p></p><h1 style="line-height: 64px;">The Gospel According to Matthew<o:p></o:p></h1><p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 32px; text-indent: 0.5in;">If gospel means “good news”, what was the good news according to Matthew? It was that the old dead religious ways of being were passing away. A new vibrant way of living was among us. Matthew presents a Jesus who is rooted and grounded in the history of Israel’s continuing story both in Jesus’ own family of origin, as well as his fulfillment of prophecy, and he continues the story in our lives. He summarizes the entire life message of Jesus by saying that he came to preach one message: “Repent for the kingdom of heaven is at hand”<a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftn4" name="_ftnref4" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11pt; line-height: 15.6933px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[4]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>. I once heard a message from Bob Hamp, LMFT, who said that “repentance” simply means to think differently afterward. As such, Jesus began to challenge the minds of all who would listen. He told parables to attempt to alter the way they thought and the way they saw God’s heart. He said, “you’ve heard… but I say…”. He demonstrated his authority to challenge the status quo by revealing his power in healing and miracles. In one section, Jesus made a point to say that he was showing he had the authority to forgive sins by demonstrating that authority through healing. Matthew does not tell us of a hero but of the first and best role model. He is inviting us into the story. Jesus not only demonstrated this power in himself alone, but he gave it to his apostles as well. This power was available to anyone who would “have faith”. Jesus frequently comments on those who have great faith, little faith, and no faith. He frequently puts the responsibility (good and bad) for outcome back on the receiver, “…your faith has made you whole”. The Good News of Matthew is that if we will hear and think differently afterward, we can enter this Kingdom of Heaven now and live a new kind of adventurous life. He goes on to tell us that there are costs and rewards associated with this adventure lifestyle. And then he ends by telling us that we have access to the power of the risen Jesus to go out into the world and make disciples just as he had. I can almost hear him say “Tag, you’re it”.<o:p></o:p></p><div><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><br clear="all" /><hr align="left" size="1" width="33%" /><!--[endif]--><div id="ftn1"><p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: 0.5in;"><a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftnref1" name="_ftn1" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10pt; line-height: 14.2667px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[1]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>&nbsp;<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;vQG1nFHW&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}Messiah},
Immerse\\uc0\\u8239{}: The Reading Bible (Carol Stream, Illinois: Tyndale House
Publishers, Inc, 2017).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Messiah,
Immerse : The Reading Bible (Carol Stream, Illinois: Tyndale House Publishers,
Inc,
2017).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:1},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:182,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/UD6M72XX&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/UD6M72XX&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:182,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;\&quot;Immerse:
Messiah is the first of six volumes of Immerse: The Reading Bible. Messiah
takes the reader on a new and unique journey through every book of the New
Testament. Each part of Messiah begins with one of the Gospels, and together,
they combine to provide the reader with enjoyable and impactful readings, all
centered on Jesus. The end result is a Bible developed for the purpose of being
read and understood, not referred to. Messiah is a new way to interact with
God's Word. Messiah delivers Scripture to the reader as it was originally
created: without chapter or verse breaks. While references are made available
for ease of finding specific scripture references, those references do not
appear within the actual text of Scripture. This makes for uninterrupted
reading of God's Word. Created with the look and feel of a paperback book, and
written using the Holy Bible New Living Translation, the most readable
translation available, Messiah enables you to easily read and understand the Bible\&quot;--&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BS2095
.N394 2017&quot;,&quot;collection-title&quot;:&quot;Immerse : the reading
Bible&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Carol Stream,
Illinois&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-1-4964-2413-6&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;502&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Tyndale
House Publishers, Inc&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Carol Stream,
Illinois&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Messiah&quot;,&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2017&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]--><i>Messiah</i>, Immerse : The Reading Bible (Carol Stream, Illinois: Tyndale House Publishers, Inc, 2017).<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p><p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: 0.5in;"><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p></div><div id="ftn2"><p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: 0.5in;"><a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftnref2" name="_ftn2" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10pt; line-height: 14.2667px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[2]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>&nbsp;<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;VH9jJ0Mr&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}Hebrew-Greek
Key Word Study Bible: Key Insights Into God\\uc0\\u8217{}s Word. NKJV New King
James Version}, Genuine Black Leather (Chattanooga, TN: AMG Publishers,
2015).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Hebrew-Greek Key Word Study Bible:
Key Insights Into God’s Word. NKJV New King James Version, Genuine Black
Leather (Chattanooga, TN: AMG Publishers,
2015).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:2},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:93,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/CUWK86LA&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/CUWK86LA&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:93,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;Genuine
Black Leather&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Chattanooga,
TN&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;13:
978-0-89957-868-2&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;English&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;2,615&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;AMG
Publishers&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Chattanooga,
TN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Hebrew-Greek Key Word Study Bible: Key
Insights Into God's Word. NKJV New King James
Version&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Key Word
NKJV&quot;,&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2015&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]--><i>Hebrew-Greek Key Word Study Bible: Key Insights Into God’s Word. NKJV New King James Version</i>, Genuine Black Leather (Chattanooga, TN: AMG Publishers, 2015).<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p></div><div id="ftn3"><p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: 0.5in;"><a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftnref3" name="_ftn3" title=""></a><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p><p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: 0.5in;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10pt; line-height: 14.2667px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[3]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span>&nbsp;<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;F3FfGKW2&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Ann
Spangler and Lois Tverberg, {\\i{}Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith}, Updated edition (Grand Rapids,
Michigan: Zondervan, 2018).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Ann Spangler
and Lois Tverberg, Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the Jewishness of
Jesus Can Transform Your Faith, Updated edition (Grand Rapids, Michigan:
Zondervan, 2018).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:3},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:186,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VFB8VX5A&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VFB8VX5A&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:186,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;Sitting
at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus takes readers on a fascinating journey, helping them
discover how learning about the Jewish world of Jesus can enrich their own
faith. This newly expanded softcover edition includes a discussion guide and a
Jewish Seder liturgy&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BT590.J8 S63
2018&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;Updated
edition&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Grand Rapids,
Michigan&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-310-33069-1&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;301&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Zondervan&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Grand
Rapids, Michigan&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your
Faith&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Sitting at the feet of Rabbi
Jesus&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Spangler&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Ann&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Tverberg&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Lois&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2018&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Ann Spangler and Lois Tverberg,&nbsp;<i>Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith</i>, Updated edition (Grand Rapids, Michigan: Zondervan, 2018).<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p></div><div id="ftn4"><p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="file:///H:/My%20Drive/TKU%20Courses%20to%20be%20reviewed%20and%20cleaned%20out/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/.Completed/Gospel%20Reading%20Matthew_Stud%20Wolfe,%20Darrell_Life%20of%20Jesus_Prof%20Moraff,%20Jason.docx#_ftnref4" name="_ftn4" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10pt; line-height: 14.2667px; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[4]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>&nbsp;<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;ZEaqroFq&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}Key
Word NKJV}.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Key Word NKJV.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:4},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:93,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/CUWK86LA&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/CUWK86LA&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:93,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;Genuine
Black Leather&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Chattanooga,
TN&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;13:
978-0-89957-868-2&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;English&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;2,615&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;AMG
Publishers&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Chattanooga,
TN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Hebrew-Greek Key Word Study Bible: Key
Insights Into God's Word. NKJV New King James
Version&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Key Word
NKJV&quot;,&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2015&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]--><i>Key Word NKJV</i>.<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:
field-end'></span><![endif]-->&nbsp;4:17<o:p></o:p></p></div></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom&nbsp;<span face="sans-serif" style="background-color: white; color: #202122; font-size: 14px;">שָׁלוֹם</span>: Live Long and Prosper!</span><br /><div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe</span></div>Storyteller | Writer | Thinker | Consultant | Freelancer | Bible Nerd
